commands.review_summary:
The PR makes the Control UI history loader adopt `chat.history`/`chat.startup` `inFlightRun` snapshots and adds unit plus browser e2e coverage for reconnect restoration.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +149. Total +183 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has a high-confidence source reproduction path: the gateway returns bounded `inFlightRun`, the TUI consumes it, but the Control UI history loader ignores it after session-switch stream clearing; I did not run tests in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because I found no actionable PR defect; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer review and required-check completion.

target.title: fix(ui): restore in-flight streaming assistant reply on Control UI reconnect (#90755)
target.body:
## Summary

- Control UI lost the in-progress assistant reply on reconnect: after switching away from a running chat and back (or reloading), the thread showed only the last committed user message while the streaming assistant response disappeared.
- The gateway already exposes the still-streaming run via the existing `chat.history` / `chat.startup` `inFlightRun` payload (built from `chatAbortControllers` / `chatRunBuffers` and bounded by `boundInFlightRunSnapshotForChatHistory`), and the TUI history loader already consumes it (`src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts`). The Control UI history loader did not.
- This patch makes the Control UI consume the same `inFlightRun` contract: the loader re-adopts the in-flight run id, rehydrates the buffered partial into `chatStream`, and sets `chatStreamStartedAt`, so the partial renders again and subsequent live deltas/final continue on the adopted run. It is symmetric with the TUI fix and adds no new reconnect subsystem or wire-protocol change.
- Adoption is guarded to only run when no run survived local stream reconciliation (`resetStream && !state.chatRunId`), so a fresher live local stream is never clobbered by a slightly stale history snapshot. Empty buffered text (e.g. Codex-style runtimes that only deliver assistant text at completion, or text dropped by the history budget) still adopts the run so the thread shows `streaming` (reading indicator) instead of looking idle.

This is the Control UI parity fix ClawSweeper called out as the best narrow solution on the issue, and the path the HTTP-only PR #90832 did not cover.

## Linked context

Closes #90755

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Control UI reconnect dropped the in-flight streaming assistant reply; the gateway-provided `chat.history`/`chat.startup` `inFlightRun` payload was never adopted by the browser history loader, so on switch-back/reload the thread showed only committed history.
- Real environment tested: local openclaw from source on Linux (real Control UI build served by Vite, driven in a real headless Chrome via the repo's Playwright e2e harness `ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts` against the mock gateway; system Chrome 138 used via `PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH` since the Playwright CDN is blocked on this host).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. `PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH=/usr/bin/google-chrome node node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts -t "in-flight streaming assistant reply on reconnect"`
  2. The browser loads `/chat`; the gateway returns committed history = only the user turn, plus `inFlightRun: { runId, text: "Streaming the summary so" }`. The test asserts the partial assistant text becomes visible in the real `.chat-thread` DOM, then emits a live final on the adopted run and asserts it renders.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):
```
 RUN  v4.1.7 /…/wt-90755

 ✓ |ui-e2e| ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts > Control UI mocked Gateway E2E > restores the in-flight streaming assistant reply on reconnect (#90755) 5838ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed | 13 skipped (14)
   Start at  15:24:34
   Duration  5.31s
```
  Negative control (proves the assertion is real behavior, not a tautology): with the loader change reverted (`git stash` of `ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts` only) and the same e2e run, the real browser never renders the partial and the test fails on exactly that visibility wait:
```
 × restores the in-flight streaming assistant reply on reconnect (#90755) 12684ms
   - waiting for locator('.chat-thread').getByText('Streaming the summary so') to be visible
     987|       await page.locator(".chat-thread").getByText("Streaming the summ…
 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 13 skipped (14)
```
- Observed result after fix: in the real browser, after reconnect the in-progress assistant reply ("Streaming the summary so") is restored and visible in the chat thread alongside the committed user turn, and the adopted run continues to receive its live final ("…the reconnect keeps it."). Without the fix the reply is absent (reproduces the reported bug).
- What was not tested: active tool-call snapshot replay across reconnect — out of scope for this issue and noted by ClawSweeper as a possible follow-up; only buffered assistant text + run adoption is addressed here. Real multi-runtime backends (Codex etc.) were not spun up; the empty-text adoption path is covered by unit tests and mirrors the already-shipped TUI behavior.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the live proof drives the real Control UI bundle in real headless Chrome, but the gateway is the repo's standard e2e mock gateway (a full live agent/gateway browser session was not run on this host). The Playwright Chromium download is blocked on this host, so the system Chrome 138 binary was used via `PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH`. The symmetric TUI fix (`src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts`) and the gateway already emitting bounded `inFlightRun` corroborate the end-to-end behavior.

## Tests and validation

- Added 4 unit tests (`ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`): buffered in-flight text restored on reconnect; empty-text in-flight run adopted (streaming, not idle); `inFlightRun` without a run id ignored; a fresher live local run not clobbered by a stale snapshot.
- Added 1 Playwright e2e test (`ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`) rendering the real Control UI in a real browser (see proof above), with a verified negative control.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts` → 121 passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts` → 136 passed (related rendering/view suites green).
- `oxlint` and `oxfmt --write` clean on all changed files.

## Risk checklist

- Scope: one Control UI source file (`ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts`, +29 incl. comments / ~9 logic lines) plus tests; no gateway, wire-protocol, or HTTP-payload changes.
- The `inFlightRun` snapshot is already bounded by the gateway (`boundInFlightRunSnapshotForChatHistory`), so no unbounded reconnect payload is introduced by this UI-side change.
- Adoption only triggers when no local run survived stream reconciliation, so it cannot override a fresher in-progress stream; empty payloads degrade to a reading indicator rather than an idle thread.
- No secrets, auth, dependency, or permission changes.
